mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Filters and returns printers from the configuration that match the specified description/state. Searches for printers in the 'pie' environment section and returns a list of dictionaries containing model and serial_number for all printers where the description matches the specified value. Enables data-driven testing by allowing test scripts to dynamically select printers based on their subscription state.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This precondition requires a printer enrolled with 50+ pages plan. The config.yaml contains printer inventory with various subscription states. This method can filter printers by state to select an appropriate enrolled printer for the test. The config includes printers with 'subscribed' state which would have active plans.

components_found_in_this_batch.constant_value: List of 5 printers with various states including subscribed printers
components_found_in_this_batch.how_to_import:
from config_loader import load_config
config = load_config('config.yaml')
printers = config['printers']['pie_environment']
components_found_in_this_batch.usage_example:
config = load_config('config.yaml')
printers = config['printers']['pie_environment']
subscribed_printer = [p for p in printers if p['description'] == 'subscribed'][0]
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: Provides printer inventory data including subscribed printers that can be used to fulfill the precondition of having an enrolled printer with an active plan.

precondition_mapping.notes: Found configuration data and helper method to select printers by state, but no actual enrollment API methods or page objects for enrollment flow. The enrollment page metadata (Enroll.json) contains UI elements for the enrollment review page but not the actual enrollment action methods. Full enrollment automation would require page objects and API helpers from other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No API helpers, verification methods, or subscription state checking utilities found in this batch. The config.yaml contains printer data but no subscription detail verification methods. Subscription verification methods likely exist in API helper or backend utility files in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Filters printers by description/state. Can be used to select printers with 'subscribed' status from the configuration.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This precondition requires subscription to be in 'subscribed' status. This method can filter the printer inventory to find printers with 'subscribed' state, helping to select appropriate test data for this precondition.

precondition_mapping.notes: Found method to filter printers by 'subscribed' state for test data selection, but no API methods to verify or set subscription status. The config contains printers with 'subscribed' description but no runtime subscription status verification utilities.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: No page objects, locators, or action methods for pause plan functionality found in this batch. The Home.json metadata file contains elements for cancellation flow (cancel-your-plan, confirm-cancellation) but not for pause plan. The close-pause-plan-button locator exists but no pause plan trigger button. Pause plan page objects and methods likely exist in other batches containing dashboard or subscription management page objects.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML configuration file from the specified path. Opens the file in read mode with UTF-8 encoding, uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary, and returns the parsed configuration dictionary.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides access to configuration data including printer inventory, URLs, credentials, and test settings needed for precondition setup and test execution.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Filters and returns printers from the configuration that match the specified description/state. Enables data-driven testing by allowing test scripts to dynamically select printers based on their subscription state.
matched_methods.usage_example:
config = load_config('config.yaml')
subscribed_printers = get_printers_by_description(config, 'subscribed')
printer = subscribed_printers[0] if subscribed_printers else None
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Helps fulfill preconditions by selecting appropriate printers with specific subscription states (subscribed, enrolled) from the test data inventory.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Session-scoped pytest fixture that configures logging for the entire test session. Sets up both file and console logging handlers with INFO level and timestamp formatting.
matched_methods.how_to_import: # This is a pytest fixture - automatically available in test files
matched_methods.usage_example:
# Fixture runs automatically due to autouse=True
# Logs will be written to file specified in config['log_file'] and console
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides logging infrastructure for test execution which would be useful for debugging this complex multi-step test with time manipulation and print simulation.

matched_helpers.helper_signature: module with load_config() and get_printers_by_description() functions
matched_helpers.what_it_does: Configuration loader utility module providing functions to load YAML configuration files and filter printer configurations by description. Serves as the interface between the config.yaml file and test scripts.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: Provides utilities to load configuration and select appropriate test data (printers with specific states) for precondition setup.

matched_helpers.helper_signature: pytest configuration module with fixtures and YAML test data loading
matched_helpers.what_it_does: Pytest configuration file providing test fixtures, command-line options, and YAML test data loading functionality. Configures logging, defines custom pytest options for YAML-driven testing, and provides fixtures for accessing YAML test data.
matched_helpers.how_to_import: # Fixtures are automatically available in pytest test files
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: Provides pytest infrastructure for YAML-driven testing and logging which could be used to structure this complex test case if it were defined in YAML format.

matched_constants.how_to_import:

login_url = config['login']['url']
matched_constants.relevance_reason: Provides the base URL for the HP Instant Ink application which is needed for navigation to Print and Payment History page.

username = config['login']['username']

password = config['login']['password']

matched_constants.how_to_import:

test_case_mappings.batch_analysis_summary: This batch contains configuration files, test infrastructure, and UI metadata for enrollment and home pages, but NO components for the Print and Payment History page which is the focus of test case C44873414. The batch provides valuable test data infrastructure (printer inventory, configuration loading, logging setup) but lacks the page objects, locators, action methods, and verification methods needed to automate the actual test steps. Key findings: (1) Configuration utilities (load_config, get_printers_by_description) can help with precondition setup by selecting appropriate test printers, (2) Base URL and credentials are available in config.yaml for navigation and authentication, (3) Printer inventory with various subscription states enables data-driven testing, (4) Screenshot directory configuration supports visual testing in step 20, (5) Pytest fixtures provide test infrastructure but no page-specific automation. All 21 test steps require page objects, locators, and methods that are NOT in this batch. The Print and Payment History page objects, billing cycle card locators, progress bar verification methods, tooltip interaction methods, print simulation APIs, and time manipulation utilities must exist in other batches containing page objects, API helpers, and UI automation utilities.
